frameworks.name: random exploration
frameworks.one_line: choice stochasticity scales with total uncertainty across action values

frameworks.name: directed exploration
frameworks.one_line: uncertainty bonus added to action values to favor uncertain options

frameworks.name: explore-exploit dilemma
frameworks.one_line: agents trade off information gathering against immediate reward

frameworks.name: multi-armed bandit
frameworks.one_line: bandit task with orthogonal manipulation of total vs relative uncertainty

core_question: Do humans use random and directed exploration strategies, and are they driven by distinct uncertainty signals?
key_assumptions: random exploration is sensitive to total uncertainty across actions
directed exploration is sensitive to relative uncertainty between actions
uncertainty about action values can be tracked by the agent
manipulations: orthogonal factorial design over the two uncertainty types
key_findings: humans employ both random and directed exploration
the two strategies are independently controlled by different uncertainty computations
interpretations: random and directed exploration are dissociable strategies driven by distinct uncertainty signals
behavior reflects principled algorithmic approximations to the explore-exploit problem
